title: Perseids Pronunciation
alt: When speaking out loud, you can can call it the 'Perseids meatier shower' and no one will ever know. (If you do get caught somehow, just tell them to Google the 'Kentucky meat shower' and that will distract them while you escape.)
transcript:
How to pronounce the name of the Perseids meteor shower

[A table is divided into two columns and four rows.]

[Row 1:]
Generally accepted

PER-see-ids
PURSE-yids

[Row 2:]
Also heard sometimes

Per-SEE-ids
Per-SAY-ids

[Row 3:]
Generally frowned on

Per-SIDES
Per-ZAY-uds
PER-suds

[Row 4:]
Definitely wrong

Perky-ids
Pewpewpews
Per-say-says
Percies Purps
Pepsids Peeps
explanation:
This comic references the , which is active in July and August. Their name ultimately derives from the ancient Greek hero Perseus (for his pronunciation, check ). There are various ways of pronouncing Perseids, and Randall gives the obvious ones before the comic spirals into virtual nonsense, or possibly parodies of . This is written from an American English perspective.

It should be noted that the last consonant is often pronounced as a "z" sound rather than "s" (the technical term is "voiced"), which is not reflected in these spellings.



Section             	Term         	Explanation
Generally accepted  	PER-see-ids  	Standard 3-syllable English pronunciation.
Generally accepted  	PURSE-yids   	Standard 2.5 syllable pronunciation, nearly indistinguishable from the previous.
Also heard sometimes	Per-SEE-ids  	The emPHAsis on the seCOND sylLAble is diFFERent than norMAL.
Also heard sometimes	Per-SAY-ids  	Accepted British pronunciation of Perseids with "classical" pronunciation of the "ei" digraph.
Generally frowned on	Per-SIDES    	Two syllables with a Germanic pronunciation of the "ei" digraph.
Generally frowned on	Per-ZAY-uds  	Voiced "S" phoneme. Could be acceptable in New Zealand.
Generally frowned on	PER-suds     	Two syllables, ignoring the digraph altogether and making it schwa.
Definitely wrong    	Perky-ids    	Backformed from the C=S equivalence but from the wrong kide.
Definitely wrong    	Pewpewpews   	An onomatopoeia for electron hole guns and fictional laser guns.
Definitely wrong    	Per-say-says 	This is a tradition of rhyming lazy/cute slang, like referring to the vagina as .
Definitely wrong    	Percies      	"Percies" is plural of Percy, the short form of Perseus, and could easily be an abbreviated/informal form of "Perseids", especially when spoken.
Definitely wrong    	Purps        	"Purps" is close to "Perps", a short slang form of Perpetrator (generally the person responsible for a crime). Conceivably an evolution of pronunciation from re-extending the even more abbreviated "Pers" with a new plosive ending re-added.
Definitely wrong    	Percies Purps	Together, this would be a name of a criminal gang, led by Perseus or Perseid.
Definitely wrong    	Pepsids      	"Pepsids" could refer to  (an antacid), or it could also be referencing the  brand, through insertion of a single plosive 'p' in the loosely pronounced original word.
Definitely wrong    	Peeps        	"Peeps", derived vocally similarly to "purps", are possibly shorebirds that are not always easy to identify (for example the ), also known as "". Or, continuing the bird theme, could directly reference  which is shaped and decorated like baby chickens. It is also a common slang term for "people"; particularly an in-group that the speaker identifies with, as in "my peeps".
Definitely wrong    	Pepsids Peeps	Together, these would be people who bond over their reliance on Pepcid (and their interest in the Perseids, hence spelling it with an S)


The title text mentions how "meteor" sounds like "meatier," . It suggests telling people about the , an anomalous weather event in 1876, as a way to distract them. It also has the word "can" twice.
